perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned and that all subsequent amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G shall be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned without the necessity of filing additional joint acquisition statements.
perturbation.changed_text: The Subscribing Parties acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned and that all subsequent amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G may be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned at their discretion without the necessity of filing additional joint acquisition statements.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'The undersigned' to 'The Subscribing Parties' introduces ambiguity. While the original text mandates that subsequent amendments SHALL be filed, the modified text makes it discretionary ('may be filed at their discretion'), creating a contradiction. The document later assumes mandatory filing, but this change now makes that assumption questionable. The term 'Subscribing Parties' and 'undersigned' are used interchangeably here, causing confusion on wether or not both terms represents the same obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT FILING STATEMENT PURSUANT TO RULE 13d-1(k) The Subscribing Parties acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned and that all subsequent amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G may be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned at their discretion without the necessity of filing additional joint acquisition statements. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. DATED: April 8, 2020 SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'may' in the changed text introduces a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text mandates that subsequent amendments must be filed, while the modified text makes filing discretionary ('may be filed at their discretion'). This creates an inconsistency because the contradicted text might be read as assuming that the Subscribing Parties are obligated to file, while the changed text gives them the option not to. Since both texts are included in the same document this misalingment leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the general nature of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the specifics or accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'completeness and accuracy' to 'general nature' introduces a contradiction. The original text requires a high standard of data integrity, while the modified version allows for a less rigorous standard. This contradiction could lead to disputes over the level of due diligence required in providing information, where the change from 'completeness and accuracy' into 'general nature' is a less strong term, creating a weakness in the requirement of quality of the information being provided. The original term in comparison imposes a high standard in comparison to the modified text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT FILING STATEMENT PURSUANT TO RULE 13d-1(k) The Subscribing Parties acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned and that all subsequent amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G may be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned at their discretion without the necessity of filing additional joint acquisition statements. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the general nature of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the specifics or accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. DATED: April 8, 2020 SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.justification: The original text emphasizes 'completeness and accuracy,' imposing a high standard for the information provided. The changed text weakens this standard by using the term 'general nature,' which is less rigorous. Since the Contradicted Text is simply the Changed Text in it's entirety, it creates a contradiction because the new text now has a weaker standard of information quality in comparison to the original text of 'completeness and accuracy,' creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.changed_text: SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Senior Director SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Legal Officer SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.explanation: Changing the job titles of Thomas Sipp across different entities (e.g., from 'Chief Financial Officer' to 'Senior Director' or 'Chief Legal Officer') introduces a contradiction regarding his authority and responsibility. This raises questions about whether he is acting in the same capacity for each entity and whether his signature holds equal weight across all filings. It is contradicting because someone with the 'Chief Financial Officer' can make desicions on financial matters. However, if the same person is the 'Chief Legal Officer' might not have the same financial authority, and viceversa for the 'Senior Director'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT FILING STATEMENT PURSUANT TO RULE 13d-1(k) The Subscribing Parties acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned and that all subsequent amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G may be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned at their discretion without the necessity of filing additional joint acquisition statements. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the general nature of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the specifics or accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. DATED: April 8, 2020 SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Senior Director SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Legal Officer SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.justification: The modified text alters Thomas Sipp's job title across different entities, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text shows Thomas Sipp with different roles (Chief Financial Officer, Senior Director, and Chief Legal Officer) simultaneously for different entities. This contradicts the idea that he has consistent authority and responsibility across all filings and introduces ambiguity about his decision-making capacity for each entity. The original text represented him as 'Chief Financial Officer' for all entities, which presented a consistent representation of his authority. The changed titles directly impact the perceived and potentially actual legal capacity of his signature. Because of this ambiguity, this is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, based on the given definition.
